channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: Do blood flow restriction exercises offer additional benefits when compared to conventional exercises in musculoskeletal rehabilitation? A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: This systematic review and meta-analysis of RCTs found that low-load exercise with blood flow restriction (LLE-BFR) offers additional benefits over low-load exercise (LLE) and comparable benefits to high-load exercise (HLE) in muscle strength, disability, and pain for musculoskeletal conditions.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Frontline agreement in diagnosis and treatment for musculoskeletal dysfunctions. A prospective, randomized, controlled study.
item.description: This RCT evaluated the agreement in diagnosis, treatment, and discharge decisions between physiotherapists and resident physicians compared with senior physicians as the reference standard in an emergency department for musculoskeletal complaints.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effects of physical exercise on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis: A systematic review.
item.description: This systematic review analyzed the effectiveness of different physical exercise interventions on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis, including randomized controlled trials.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Quantifying Physical Activity Through Step Count Estimation Across Multiple Wearable Sensor Configurations: A Validation Study in Post-Stroke and Healthy Adults.
item.description: This validation study identified the most accurate protocol for measuring stride-count in 18 post-stroke and 18 healthy individuals, comparing different wearable sensors, their placement, and data processing against a gold-standard instrumented treadmill.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Development of a Core Patient-Reported Outcome (Measures) Set for Pediatric Physical Therapy.
item.description: This study developed a core set of 9 relevant patient-reported outcomes (PROs) and corresponding measures (PROMs) for pediatric physical therapy, grouped into 3 domains: functioning, well-being, and participation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Impact of Mandatory Bundled Payment on Prehabilitation Delivery for Lower Extremity Joint Replacement.
item.description: This difference-in-differences analysis of 62,217 Medicare beneficiaries in the US determined the effect of bundled payment for inpatient lower extremity joint replacement (LEJR) on prehabilitation delivery.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The effectiveness of cardiovascular rehabilitation for transient ischaemic attack and mild stroke: A randomised controlled trial.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial of 140 TIA/mild stroke patients found that a 6-week cardiovascular rehabilitation program significantly improved 6-minute walk test distance by 30 meters (p < 0.05) and moderate-to-vigorous physical activity over 6 months compared to usual care.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Prevalence of eating disorders and disordered eating in athletes: a systematic review and meta-analysis
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of studies from PubMed/MEDLINE, Scopus, Web of Science, and SportDiscus estimated the prevalence of eating disorders and disordered eating among athletes across training levels and assessment methods, identifying associated moderators.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Patient Engagement, Clinical Outcomes, and Satisfaction of an Advanced Orthopedic Virtual Physical Therapy Model: A Retrospective Cohort Study.
item.description: A retrospective cohort study of 137 orthopedic patients found that an advanced virtual physical therapy model achieved a 70.9% care completion rate, exceeding national benchmarks, with 96% of completers achieving Minimal Clinically Important Differences in functional improvements.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Identifying Gaps in Rehabilitation Strategies for Chronic Stroke: A Systematic Review of Evidence in Patients With and Without Nerve Transfer.
item.description: This systematic review aimed to compare upper limb rehabilitation strategies and outcomes in chronic stroke patients with versus without contralateral C7 nerve transfer and identify key evidence gaps.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Subtype-specific optimal cut-off values of the Berg Balance Scale for predicting independent walking in inpatient stroke rehabilitation: a multicentre cohort study.
item.description: A multicenter retrospective cohort study of 565 stroke patients determined subtype-specific optimal cut-off values of the Berg Balance Scale for predicting independent walking on discharge after inpatient rehabilitation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The psychosocial impacts of having eating and drinking difficulties after stroke: a qualitative evidence synthesis.
item.description: A qualitative evidence synthesis of 22 studies (645 informants) identified two overarching domains—psychological and social impacts—of eating and drinking difficulties after stroke, affecting emotional well-being and social participation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Sarcopenia Increases Adjacent Segment Degeneration Risk within 3 Years of Anterior Cervical Discectomy and Fusion.
item.description: An exploratory retrospective cohort study of 333 ACDF patients found sarcopenia to be a significant predictor for adjacent segment degeneration within 3 years of surgery.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Multi-Domain Benefits of Exergaming for Sarcopenia Management in Institutionalized Older Adults: Physical, Psychological, and Social Outcomes.
item.description: A pragmatic quasi-experimental study found a 12-week exergaming intervention (Nintendo Switch Ring Fit Adventure) in 36 institutionalized older adults with sarcopenia significantly improved physical, psychological, and social outcomes compared to a control group.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Incidence of injury and illness at the Paris 2024 Paralympic Games: post-pandemic trends and sport-specific risks
item.description: A prospective surveillance study at the Paris 2024 Paralympic Games monitored 4450 athletes over 14 days, reporting 535 injuries (incidence 8.6 per 1000 athlete days) and identifying taekwondo and football 5-a-side as high-risk sports.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: GaitEncoder: A Foundation Model of Gait Kinematics for Diverse Clinical Applications and Pathologies
item.description: GaitEncoder is a generative foundation model of walking kinematics that enables diverse downstream clinical tasks across various patient populations using clinically accessible smartphone video-based gait analysis, aggregated from eight gait datasets (6500+ participants).
item.author: medRxiv

item.title: Prehabilitation Improves Surgical Outcomes in Older Adults Undergoing Spinal Fusion: Study
item.description: A randomized clinical trial found that a multimodal prehabilitation program before spinal fusion surgery in older adults was associated with lower postoperative complications and shorter hospital stays compared to standard care, though control group patients showed better physical function at 90 days.
item.author: Medical Dialogues

item.title: Use and safety of corticosteroid injections in joints and musculoskeletal soft tissue: guidelines from the American Society of Regional Anesthesia and Pain Medicine, the American Academy of Pain Medicine, the American Society of Interventional Pain Physicians, and the International Pain and Spine Intervention Society
item.description: New guidelines from multiple American pain societies address the use and safety of corticosteroid injections in joints and musculoskeletal soft tissues, highlighting that recommended effective doses are lower than currently used by clinicians and emphasizing short-term benefits with mixed long-term relief.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Immediate Weight-Bearing and Early Ambulation Rehabilitation After Femoral Neck System Fixation for Undisplaced Femoral Neck Fractures: A Prospective Randomized Controlled Trial
item.description: A prospective randomized controlled trial investigated immediate weight-bearing and early ambulation rehabilitation after femoral neck system fixation for undisplaced femoral neck fractures.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Prevention strategies and modifiable risk factors for upper extremity injury: a systematic review and meta-analysis for the female, woman and girl Athlete Injury pRevention (FAIR) consensus
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis on prevention strategies and modifiable risk factors for upper extremity injury in female athletes, contributing to the Female, Woman and Girl Athlete Injury pRevention (FAIR) consensus.
item.author: BJSM
